ground_truth.title: Living Room Decorating Dos & Don'ts: Easy Fixes for Common Mistakes
ground_truth.author: Casey Finn
ground_truth.main_content:
While I share lots of DIY projects here on the blog, I think the most common questions I receive from readers revolve around decorating. How do I choose the right piece for my space? What size rug do I buy? Where do I shop for accessories?

That's why I created this Decorating 101 series. I'm certainly not an expert, but I have learned a lot in my nine years of blogging about how, and how not, to bring a space together. I've created individual blog posts for each room in your house to act as resources as you work to decorate and style each space.

Let me first say, when it comes to decorating, there aren't any exact rules! Every space, home, and family is different, so my words are not the be-all, end-all for decorating a space. Instead, I hope these insights can guide you as you consider your layout, large furniture purchases, and how to bring the entire look together.

Living Room Dos:

Buy a Large Rug: You've heard it from me a million times, rugs that are too small are a big no-no. In your living room, you'll probably need a big rug. You want at least the front legs of the sofa and chairs to rest on top of it.

Have Enough Seating: Most living rooms have couches or sectionals, but don't forget about additional seating. Adding a chair or two can make the space much more conversation-friendly.

Use Secret Storage: I've been in many living rooms that have way too much stuff everywhere. Instead, only highlight items that you really love and keep everything else hidden away.

Buy the Right Coffee Table: If you are buying a coffee table for your space, make sure it is the correct size and scale. It should be about 1/2 the length of your sofa and within 4" higher or lower than the height of the sofa.

Incorporate Side Tables: A lot of people forget to add side tables to their living rooms, but I think it can add so much to a space.

Use a Console Table: Another table you may want to add to your space is a console table, especially if your couch is "floating" in the middle of a room.

Living Room Don'ts:

Keep the Matching Couch Pillows: Do you know those "free" pillows that came with your couch? Now is the time to donate those forever.

Buy a Living Room Set: I know it's easy to just head to a furniture store and buy an entire matching living room set. Again, please don't do that!

Forget Lighting: Lamps are a must for any living room! Definitely don't forget to add table lamps or floor lamps to your space.

Neglect Walkways: You definitely want to leave enough room to walk between all of the furniture in your living room. A good estimate is about 16" to 18" between the sofa and coffee table.

Do the Hot Dog Fold: Instead of the hot dog fold for throw blankets, we suggest keeping your blankets in a basket, folding a blanket neatly on the side of the couch, or draping it.

Neglect Scale for Art + Shelves: Another thing I see frequently is art or shelves that are too small hanging over a sofa. Instead, make sure your art is large enough and is hung at the proper height.

Living Room Decorating - Save vs. Splurge:

Where to Splurge: The biggest investment in a living room will likely be your sofa and that's a-okay! If you're going to be sitting on it every single day then it better be comfy. Another place I suggest splurging is on secret storage. Custom built-ins are expensive, but they can make a living room!

Where to Save: Most of the other items in your living room don't need to break the bank. Accessories, throws, pillows...I almost always buy those items at HomeGoods and Target.
